detaileddescription.para: When the CPP symbol TC_RUNID is defined (for example, from the compile environment), print the defined string RunID: <TC_RUNID> when called (TC_END_REPORT() will also call it).
This is used mainly when automating the execution and running of multiple test cases, to verify that the expected image is being executed (as sometimes the targets fail to flash or reset properly).
TC_RUNID is any string, that will be converted to a string literal.

memberdef.name: TC_SUITE_START
memberdef.initializer: do { \ TC_SUITE_PRINT("Running TESTSUITE %s\n", name); \ PRINT_LINE; \ } while (false)

memberdef.name: TC_SUITE_END
TC_END_REPORT
memberdef.initializer: do { \ PRINT_LINE; \ TC_PRINT_RUNID; \ TC_END(result, \ "PROJECT EXECUTION %s\n", \ (result) == TC_PASS ? "SUCCESSFUL" : "FAILED"); \ TC_END_POST(result); \ } while (false)

memberdef.name: TC_CMD_DEFINE
memberdef.initializer: int cmd_##name(int argc, char *argv[]) \ { \ TC_START(__func__); \ name(); \ TC_END_RESULT(TC_PASS); \ return 0; \ }
